stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, enhancing local AI capabilities on Mac hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating an ongoing development and optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Elon Musk tries again to escape FTC audits of X data handling
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing renewed scrutiny from the FTC regarding X's data handling practices.
Public commentary suggests a lack of trust in Musk's ability to safeguard user privacy on the platform.
The story highlights ongoing regulatory challenges for X concerning compliance and data security.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: The skeptic’s guide to humanoid robots going viral on the Internet
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The article addresses the potential for viral robot demonstrations to create skewed public expectations.
It serves as a critical look at how media perception might misrepresent current robotic capabilities.
The topic centers on the gap between public hype and the actual state of humanoid robotics.

stories.title: These LLMs are the best at resisting Russian propaganda
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Estonian government conducted a benchmark test on various Large Language Models (LLMs).
The test specifically evaluated the models' ability to resist Russian propaganda and 'strategic narratives'.
The findings highlight which LLMs demonstrate superior resilience against state-sponsored disinformation.

stories.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Attackers successfully targeted and downloaded encrypted password vaults from Dashlane users.
The attack strategy involved targeting a large volume of users to increase the probability of a successful breach.
The story highlights a significant security vulnerability affecting a major password management service.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, focusing adaptation on 'long-tail' components.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Addresses a critical challenge in humanoid robotics: bridging the gap between high-level task planning and low-level whole-body control.
Introduces 'HANDOFF,' a novel, compact, and explicit interface designed to be intuitive and general for diverse manipulation skills.
Utilizes distilled complementary teachers to achieve effective whole-body control, moving beyond traditional dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new benchmark designed to study the evolution of AI authorship signals.
Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection, which often only analyzes final outputs rather than the co-editing process.
Focuses on the complex reality of documents resulting from progressive human-AI co-editing workflows.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: Addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs).
Proposes Supervised Memory Training (SMT) to bypass the need for recurrent credit propagation.
Aims to improve the learning of long-range associations in RNNs by enhancing parallelism and mitigating gradient issues.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning for reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve training efficiency.
The work focuses on overcoming limitations of existing methods like GRPO, which are often Monte Carlo-based.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method called 'Self-Augmenting Retrieval' for Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
It leverages the discarded, low-confidence tokens during the parallel denoising process as a valuable lookahead signal.
This signal enhances Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by enabling the retrieval of stronger evidence during text generation.

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method incurs no inference overhead as preconditioned weights can be merged back into the original architecture.

stories.title: Why AI Agents Fail at Real Browser Automation
stories.author: hadil
bullet_points: The article addresses the current limitations of AI agents when attempting complex, real-world browser automation tasks.
It discusses the technical hurdles that cause AI agents to fail in dynamic web environments.
The piece promotes a specific solution, 'BrowserAct,' as a fix for these automation failures.

stories.title: Inference Theft: Your AI Endpoint Is Someone Else's Free Model
stories.author: morganwilliscloud
bullet_points: Addresses the emerging security threat of 'inference theft' targeting AI endpoints.
Proposes practical defense mechanisms including bot detection and guardrails.
Recommends operational strategies like cost-aware routing and budget controls for protection.

stories.title: AI needs a brake pedal before the next model jump
stories.author: jenueldev
bullet_points: The article emphasizes the critical need for safety controls in advanced AI models.
These controls must allow users to slow, scope, or completely stop powerful AI systems.
The discussion frames AI safety as a practical engineering requirement rather than purely theoretical.
